text: There is something unsettling about the tilting glass and sterile light of the hallway. Entering the next space, vibrant with color and digital displays, I feel a renewed curiosity. Here, ideas and innovations are celebrated, but I also sense a tension—a reminder from the display that perception is slippery. I am engaged but wary, aware of how technology shapes and reframes my understanding. The juxtaposition of clarity and confusion invites philosophical reflection, and I feel both stimulated and unsettled by the possibilities.
